questions.text: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), health is defined as a state of complete physical, mental, and ____ well-being.
Communicable diseases are caused by pathogens and can spread from one person to another.
Disease-causing organisms such as bacteria
Which type of organism is the causal agent of Malaria?
Antibiotics are effective medicines used to treat diseases caused by viruses.
The natural ability of our body to fight against diseases is known as ____.
Who discovered the first vaccine in the late 1700s to protect people from smallpox?
Non-communicable diseases (NCDs) like diabetes and hypertension can spread from an infected person to a healthy person through air.
Penicillin
questions.type: the first antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections

questions.text: A measurable indicator of a disease that can be seen by others or a doctor (such as fever or rash) is known as a ____.
In Indian scientific heritage, which term refers to following a daily routine to maintain a health balance?
Diseases that persist for a long time (typically more than 3 months) are referred to as chronic diseases.
An insect or animal that carries and transmits a pathogen from an infected individual to another is called a ____.
Which pioneering biomedical researcher studied the links between hormones, viruses, and cancer in India?
Vaccines are curative treatments that help cure a patient once they have already fallen sick.
The first vaccine discovered in human history was developed to protect against the disease ____.
Which of the following diseases infects the digestive tract and is caused by roundworms?
The phenomenon where bacteria survive and multiply despite treatment with an antibiotic is called antibiotic resistance.
The modern index used to measure and indicate how clean or polluted the surrounding ambient air is called ____.
Which Indian scientist played a key role in developing the Rotavirus vaccine to protect children from diarrhoea?
According to the ICMR report shown on the bulletin board, what percentage of India's population is diabetic?
Regular handwashing with soap can reduce infections by 50%.
What was the traditional method called in India to protect against smallpox before modern vaccines, which involved scratching the skin with material from a sore?
The traditional practitioners who performed smallpox variolation in India were known as teekedaars.
Which district in Odisha was featured in the case study for a highly successful community-led sanitation campaign?
According to the bulletin board data, what percentage of Indians are currently obese?
Under the Vaccine Maitri initiative
questions.type: India supplied over 24 crore doses of vaccines to more than 100 countries.

questions.text: Besides Ayurveda, what other traditional medicine systems are mentioned as being historically used in India for managing everyday health problems?
If drinking water gets contaminated by human excreta, which set of waterborne diseases can spread?
According to the bulletin board 'diet mantra', by what percentage should people aim to reduce oil in their diet?
